content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs C ouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
December 21,2005
C. April Boling, CPA
7185 Navajo Road, Suite L
San Diego, CA 92119
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-05-188
Dear Ms. Boling:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ,,Act,,).1
QUESTION
Friends to Re-elect Richman transferred its remaining funds to Richman for
Treasurer (ID 1263366) on a LIFO basis in December 2004. May Richman for Treasurer
now transfer the same funds back to Friends to Re-Elect Richman to pay net debt
identified after the December transfer and attribute the funds to the same contributors that
originally contributed to the Friends to Re-Elect Richman committee?
CONCLUSION
The Richman for Treasurer Committee may transfer funds using either the LIFO
or FIFO accounting method to pay the debt of the Friends to Re-Elect Richman
committee once it is reopened pursuant to regulation lg404.L However, the
contributions must be applied to the contributors to the Richman for Treasurer
committee, using a LIFO or FIFO method.
FACTS
The facts are taken from your prior advice request, Boling Advice Lette4No. A-
05-1 10
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appeffat Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-05-188
Page No. 2
"Friends to Re-elect Richman (rD 1252000) had no net debt after
the November 2,2004 election. After all bills known at the time were
paid, Friends to Re-elect Richman transferred its remaining funds in the
amount of $32,800.27 to Richman for Treasurer (rD 1263366) on a LIFO
basis in December 2004, and then closed the committee.
'You state that in February 200s,the existence of an additional
$1,500 debt to Friends to Re-elect Richman was made known to the
committee. You understand that the committee Friends to Re-elect
Richman maybe reopened pursuant to regulation 18404.1, subdivision
(iX5). That application will be made upon receipt of a response to this
request for advice."
ANALYSIS
In the prior letter to you on this issue (Boling Advice Letter,No. A-05-110), we
advised:
"[w]here a committee has no reason to believe an outstanding bill
is forthcoming and transfers its balance of funds and closes the committee,
we characteize anew bill to be net debt of the committee. As a result, the
committee may reopen to raise funds to pay the debt. Funds may be raised
not only to cover the outstanding debt but also to cover administrative
costs associated with raising the funds, complying with reporting
requirements and winding down the committee." (Reg. 1g531.61, subd.
(d).) [Footnote omitted.]
The letter also advised:
"Section 85306, subdivision (a), allows candidates to transfer
funds from one controlled committee to another of his or her controlled
committees for elective state office under certain circumstances. This
includes transfers of funds to pay debts of the committee receiving the
funds. (See Bauer Advice Letter, No. A-04-179.) The funds -rsi b"
attributed to specific contributors to the committee making the transfer
using a 'last in, first out' or 'first in, first out' accounting method. Thus, if
the committee so wishes, the Richman for Treasurer committee may
transfer funds using either the LIFo or FIFO accounting method to pay the
debt of the Friends to Re-Elect Richman committee once it is reopened
pursuant to regulation 18404.1." [Footnote omitted]
Implicit in this advice was that the contributions transferred to Friends to Re-Elect
Richman to pay the debt must be attributable to contributors to the Richman for Treasurer
Committee. It is not allowable to use a method other than LIFO or FIFO as applied to
the contributors to Richmanfor Treasurer.


File No. A-05-188
Page No. 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By:
ohn W. Wallace
sistant General Counsel
Division
